title: Actually set version for podman module / pypodman
body:
The environment variable wasn't set, giving 0.0.0

It is a still a problem if you use python3 to build,
rather than make. You *need* to set $PODMAN_VERSION,
or your module and packages won't have the version.

comments.body:
Also, these versions (`0.0.4`) is not in sync with the rpm versions (`0.7.3`)

https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/contrib/python/podman/Makefile#L3
https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/contrib/python/pypodman/Makefile#L3

https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/contrib/spec/python-podman.spec.in#L28

PODMAN_VERSION used to be set in the root Makefile, but that has been removed. The spec file sets PODMAN_VERSION when building the rpm so that is still good.  

These changes LGTM

@jwhonce : is pypodman supposed to have the same version as podman/libpod ? (e.g. `v0.11.1.1`)
